findings.title: Structure: CG-BGID-001 deviates from its compliance-gap template
findings.detail: “Remediation” long at 54 words (template 8–40)

findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-BGID-001
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-BGID-001 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.
findings.resolutionNote: Added exception transition PS-BGID-006 → EX-BGID-001 (SWIFT NAK received on delivery)

findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-BGID-002
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-BGID-002 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.

findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-BGID-003
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-BGID-003 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.

findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-BGID-004
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-BGID-004 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.

findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-BGID-005
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-BGID-005 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.

findings.title: Orphan exception: EX-BGID-006
findings.detail: No process-step `transitions` flow into EX-BGID-006 — nothing in the documented flow reaches this exception.

findings.title: PS-BGID-001 (Application Intake) has no control linked
findings.detail: PS-BGID-001 has no control element pointing to it. All other steps have at least one control (e.g. CP-BGID-003 → PS-BGID-002, CP-BGID-002 + CP-BGID-005 → PS-BGID-004, CP-BGID-001 → PS-BGID-005, CP-BGID-004 → PS-BGID-007). The intake step accepts applications, sets channel and data-quality expectations, yet has no compensating control.

findings.title: Approval ceiling role mismatch — Head of Trade Finance is :C not :A for EUR 5M threshold
findings.detail: PS-BGID-005 (Issuance Approval) states 'Guarantees above EUR 5 million additionally require sign-off by the Head of Trade Finance; this threshold is system-enforced'. However, ROLE-BGID-003 (Head of Trade Finance) is marked :C (consulted) in the RACI for PS-BGID-005, not :A (accountable). The role that holds the approval-ceiling authority owns the decision and must be :A.

findings.title: Touchpoint sets undefined client expectation but step claims informal SLA
findings.detail: JT-BGID-003 (Legal Wording Feedback) states 'the wait is undefined, no interim status updates are provided, and the client cannot estimate the issuance date'. PS-BGID-003 declares an SLA of '2 business days (bespoke wording — informal desk convention; not tracked in TFS)'. The touchpoint experience and the SLA entry are contradictory. Is the informal SLA only used internally and never communicated to the client, or should the touchpoint record be updated to reflect it?

findings.title: REG-BGID-002 (URDG 758) has no control mapped via regulatedBy
findings.detail: REG-BGID-002 requires guarantee wording to comply with URDG 758 rules. The 'How it is met' block cites version-controlled templates at PS-BGID-003, but no control element carries regulatedBy: REG-BGID-002. An auditor reviewing regulatory obligations would find no control closing this obligation.

findings.title: REG-BGID-003 (CRR Capital Treatment) has no control mapped via regulatedBy
findings.detail: REG-BGID-003 requires correct CRR Article 111 capital treatment with CCF application for off-balance-sheet exposures. The 'How it is met' block is marked 'to be confirmed with SME' and no control references this regulation. An auditor reviewing capital adequacy would find an uncontrolled regulatory obligation.

findings.title: REG-BGID-009 (DORA ICT Risk Management) has no control mapped via regulatedBy
findings.detail: REG-BGID-009 requires documented ICT risk management and resilience testing for systems supporting critical functions. The 'How it is met' block describes external system governance and explicitly notes it is not a process control. No control element references REG-BGID-009.

findings.title: REG-BGID-010 (GDPR) has no control mapped via regulatedBy
findings.detail: REG-BGID-010 governs personal data handling at intake (KYC, application forms). The 'How it is met' block contains policy statements about lawful basis and data minimisation but no control element enforces GDPR-compliant data handling at PS-BGID-001 or elsewhere via regulatedBy.

findings.title: REG-BGID-012 (COREP Supervisory Reporting) has no control mapped via regulatedBy
findings.detail: REG-BGID-012 mandates COREP C 07.00 reporting of off-balance-sheet guarantee exposures. The 'How it is met' block describes a Finance/Regulatory Reporting feed 'to be confirmed' but no control closes this obligation. An auditor reviewing regulatory reporting would find an uncontrolled obligation.

findings.title: PP-BGID-001 has no friction-point linked to it
findings.detail: PP-BGID-001 (Facility Limit Shortfall Stalls Applications) is a documented pain-point with no friction-point referencing it via a painPoint link. FP-BGID-001 and FP-BGID-002 both link to PP-BGID-002, establishing the expected pattern. PP-BGID-001 surfaces at PS-BGID-002 but no friction-point articulates the client-journey moment where the shortfall is experienced.

findings.title: FP-BGID-003 missing pain-point link
findings.detail: FP-BGID-003 (Collateral Booking Status Opaque to Client) does not link to any pain-point via the painPoint field. II-BGID-005 addresses it, confirming it is a real issue, but the friction-point is disconnected from the pain-point hierarchy. Without a painPoint link, the client journey trace is broken.

findings.title: FP-BGID-004 missing pain-point link
findings.detail: FP-BGID-004 (MT760 Delivery Not Acknowledged to Client) does not link to any pain-point via the painPoint field. II-BGID-004 addresses it, confirming it is a real issue, but the friction-point is disconnected from the pain-point hierarchy.

findings.title: II-BGID-001 claims to address FP-BGID-002 (queue visibility) but body only addresses SLA commitment
findings.detail: II-BGID-001 (AI Wording Pre-Screener) lists FP-BGID-002 (No Queue Visibility During Legal Wording Review) in its addresses field, but the idea body describes a pre-screening mechanism with a committed SLA — which directly addresses FP-BGID-001 and PP-BGID-002. The body contains no mention of queue visibility, application tracking, or client-facing status updates. The FP-BGID-002 link is a content/relation mismatch.

findings.title: II-BGID-004 claims to address FP-BGID-004 (MT760 delivery ack) but body is about application submission
findings.detail: II-BGID-004 (ICC-SWIFT API Application Channel) lists FP-BGID-004 (MT760 Delivery Not Acknowledged to Client) in its addresses field, but the body focuses entirely on structured ERP-to-bank application submission and mandatory field completeness. The body does not mention MT760 delivery, SWIFT acknowledgement routing, or client notification of beneficiary bank receipt. The FP-BGID-004 link is a content/relation mismatch.

findings.title: PS-BGID-007 (Collateral Confirmation) mentions system interactions but has empty systems list
findings.detail: PS-BGID-007 body describes confirming cash collateral receipt and recording the blocked-funds confirmation — both are IT system interactions. INT-BGID-004 and INT-BGID-005 explicitly target the Facility/Credit System at this step. The step should list at minimum SYS-BGID-002 (Trade Finance System) and SYS-BGID-005 (Facility & Credit System / Murex).

findings.title: All six systems missing structured DORA/ICT-mapping fields in frontmatter
findings.detail: SYS-BGID-001 through SYS-BGID-006 all lack structured YAML fields for criticality, vendor, dataClassification, rtoBand and rpoBand. These values appear only in unstructured prose in the Purpose block. DORA Article 28 and ICT-risk mapping tools require machine-readable fields. What are the correct values for each system?
